Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1287457615|1287457615]] by [[Special:Contributions/PrimalMustelid|PrimalMustelid]] ([[User talk:PrimalMustelid|talk]]) Per the 1RR rule, I'm self-reverting by request. My argument still applies, but I'll avoid bending this rule.

Per [[WP:NOTTRUTH]], what matters most here is verifiability, not what you consider to be true. And what's well-verified is that Netanyahu is considered to be authoritarian and in league with similar politicians like Orban. Deflecting these sources with "leftism" or "antisemitism" is like pulling the "anti-western" or "Islamophobic" cards regarding sources of Erdogan's authoritarianism.

'Multiple sources' does not and cannot mean it is an actual fact. These 'sources' come mostly from anti-Semites or his political opponents. Just like the anti-Semitic "[[Palestinian genocide accusation|Palestinian genocide]]" tirades.
/* Political positions */ not POV issue, backed up by multiple sources
used (more) attributed phrasing for now. I suggest citations for the article body be discussed in the [[Talk:Benjamin Netanyahu|Talk page]]
These are all biased POV statements that cannot be proven in any way whatsoever. It's a fact that the State of Israel is one of the few liberal democratic states in the region.
replace dead citation links with live links

No real explanations have been provided for these POV tags - many sources have abundantly pointed out Netanyahu's authoritarian style of government and attempts to undermine other government branches to consolidate power not to mention that he is literally allied with fellow authoritarians from other countries.
